news.title: Prime Offices Fund acquires 51% stake in 1.1 million sq ft office asset in Pune
news.para: PRIME Offices Fund has acquired a significant stake in a large office development in Pune. This move expands PRIME's office portfolio to nearly 3.8 million square feet. The fund manager, NCW, is a joint venture between Nuvama Asset Management and Cushman & Wakefield. This follows a recent major acquisition in Chennai.

news.title: The evolution of home loan insurance in India
news.para: In India, home loan insurance is undergoing a transformation. It has become an essential financial ally for homeowners. With more affordable options, borrowers can now shield their families from the burden of debt. The ability to personalize coverage allows individuals to tailor protection to their unique circumstances.

news.title: Ahmedabad development body can now allow development in agri zone at Gokuldham
news.para: After a dispute suspended permissions in 2014, the Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority chairman can now issue development and revised development permissions for agricultural land within the Gokuldham township. This authority covers over 10 lakh square metres in Sanand taluka, following a decade-long approval process for the township's layout and subsequent land integrations.

news.title: Smart city projects near completion in 10 Uttar Pradesh cities
news.para: Uttar Pradesh has achieved a significant milestone in its Smart City mission, with 99% of its 682 sanctioned projects now commissioned. Despite initial delays due to irregularities, all allocated funds have been utilized, and the remaining projects are expected to be operational by March 14. This success paves the way for potential central government funding for further city upgrades.

news.title: SC issues notice to Goa government, developer for construction of villas at Siolim
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued notices to the Goa government and a project developer. This follows a challenge to a High Court order that dismissed a plea against building eight luxury villas at Oxel in Siolim. The petitioner argues the project threatens the 363-year-old Oxel spring, a vital water source for residents facing a deficit. The court's decision is awaited.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body to take help of brokers, marketing cell to sell 1,700 flats
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority plans to sell over 1,700 unsold houses. Professional brokers will be hired to find buyers. A marketing cell will also be established. This strategy aims to generate over Rs 500 crore. The initiative will not apply to EWS flats. This follows a similar state government scheme.

news.title: HC asks Greater Noida authority to restore plot it cancelled nine years ago
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has reinstated a Rs 100 crore farmhouse plot to SDS Infratech, overturning a nine-year-old cancellation by the Greater Noida Authority. The court directed the authority to decide on the developer's zero-period relief claim within four months, quashing the 2017 cancellation order.

news.title: Cafes, restaurants on Noida abadi land bypassing fire NOCs, building bylaws, parking norms
news.para: Popular Noida nightlife hubs, built on abadi lands, are operating without fire safety clearances, posing a significant risk. Despite warnings from residents about narrow access and overloaded connections, authorities face limitations due to relaxed norms for establishments under 500 sq m, creating a dangerous regulatory gap.

news.title: Delhi mayor urges residents to clear dues under property tax amnesty scheme before March 31
news.para: Delhi Mayor Raja Iqbal Singh is encouraging property owners to use the SUNIYO 2025-26 property tax amnesty scheme. This scheme offers a waiver on past interest and penalties for dues before the financial year 2020-21. The deadline for this opportunity is March 31, 2026. Over 1.8 lakh taxpayers have already benefited.

news.title: Housing market in UK slowed noticeably in January: Bank of England data
news.para: January witnessed a dip in British mortgage approvals, marking a two-year low, as per the latest figures from the Bank of England. This decline paints a stark contrast to the rapid increase in consumer credit. Interestingly, house prices have experienced an unexpected surge. Despite these fluctuations, economic activity showed signs of resilience in January.

news.title: Haryana budget: 100% waiver on outstanding interest till Mar 2025 on property tax
news.para: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini unveiled a budget with significant relief measures. Property tax interest waivers and free water supply for many households are key highlights. The budget also earmarks funds for urban greening and waste-to-energy plants. Initiatives to boost industrial activity, including a land lease policy and a new fund, were also announced.

news.title: Government mandate on green steel may cut up to 29.7 MtCO₂ by 2030: CII Study
news.para: A 26% green steel mandate in public projects could unlock 16 MTPA demand by FY2030, while a 37% threshold may create a 24 MTPA market and cut up to 29.7 MtCO₂ emissions, according to a CII study.

news.title: YEIDA plans FinTech hub on 500 acres near Noida airport
news.para: Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority is proposing a 500-acre FinTech Hub in Sector 11, near Noida International Airport, inviting expressions of interest for development. This world-class financial ecosystem aims to attract core financial services, IT, and digital operations. The project, slated for development through various models, will position Noida as a significant FinTech destination.

news.title: Kanpur civic body approves uniform mutation fee from March 1
news.para: Kanpur Municipal Corporation has reduced the mutation fee to a flat Rs 6,500, effective March 1, 2026. This change, approved after a 63-day process and addressing public objections, replaces the previous 1% of property value based on DM's circle rate. The maximum fee will now be Rs 10,000 for properties exceeding Rs 50 lakh.

news.title: After HC setback, Ghaziabad residents seek political fix to property tax hike
news.para: Ghaziabad residents and former councillors are exploring political avenues and a potential Supreme Court challenge after the Allahabad High Court upheld the Municipal Corporation's property tax hike. The revision, which aligns tax slabs with district magistrate circle rates, has led to a significant increase in property taxes for many households, sparking widespread protests.

news.title: India’s green building market at inflection point: What changed in 2025 and what comes next
news.para: In 2025, Indian real estate's sustainability focus shifted from design to operational performance, driven by energy demands, climate risks, and investor preferences. This transition is reshaping asset strategies, capital allocation, and development priorities across office, industrial, and retail sectors, emphasizing measurable building performance over aspirational claims.

news.title: Gaurs Group to invest ₹100 crore to set up pre-cast manufacturing facility in Noida
news.para: Gaurs Group has partnered with Elematic India to establish a ₹100 crore precast manufacturing facility in Noida. This facility will produce advanced concrete components, aiming to reduce project delivery timelines by six months to a year. The group has also placed a significant order for modular bathrooms and kitchen pods.

news.title: Big FM forays into real estate sector
news.para: Big FM has launched Big FM Realty, entering the Indian real estate market. The company targets ₹3,500 to ₹4,000 crore revenue within three years.  A significant development is planned in Bahraich, Uttar Pradesh, on approximately 80 acres. This mixed-use township is expected to generate ₹1,200 crore in revenue.

news.title: Sushmita Sen and her mother lease out Mumbai apartment for ₹1.95 lakh per month
news.para: Actress Sushmita Sen and her mother Subhra Sen have leased their Mumbai apartment. The rental agreement begins at ₹1.95 lakh monthly. This deal includes two car parking spaces. The lease spans 36 months with rent increments. The total rental value over three years is ₹73.76 lakh. This transaction was registered in February 2026.
